game_info.title: Traffic Run
game_info.concept: **Traffic Run:** Players control a single vehicle positioned at a stop line before an intersection, using a tap-and-hold mechanic to accelerate forward and releasing the tap to brake and stop. The core objective is to safely cross multi-lane intersections where continuous streams of AI-controlled traffic flow perpendicularly, requiring precise timing to navigate through gaps in the traffic. Each level presents a distinct intersection layout, starting with 2-lane roads and progressing to 4-lane or more complex junctions with varying traffic speeds (e.g., AI cars travel at 10-20 units/second) and densities. Successfully crossing an intersection completes the current level and awards coins (e.g., 10 coins per level), which can be used to unlock new vehicle skins or visual upgrades that do not affect gameplay mechanics. The game features an endless level progression, with difficulty scaling through increased traffic volume, faster AI vehicles, and more intricate intersection designs, and the run ends immediately upon any collision with another vehicle, requiring a restart of the current level.
game_info.description: Navigate your vehicle through dangerous intersections filled with fast-moving traffic. Hold SPACE to accelerate forward and release to brake. Time your movements carefully to slip through gaps in the cross-traffic without colliding. Each successful crossing earns you coins and advances you to more challenging intersections with faster traffic and more lanes. One collision ends your run!
game_info.controls:
SPACE: Hold to accelerate forward, release to brake
ENTER: Start the game from the title screen
ESC: Pause/unpause during gameplay
R: Restart after game over

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic game initialization, vehicle spawning, traffic generation, and simple forward movement. This test validates that the game starts correctly, the player vehicle appears at the starting position, and AI traffic flows across the intersection as expected.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The automated player will hold SPACE for 2 seconds to accelerate forward slightly, then release for 1 second. This pattern repeats to test the acceleration and braking mechanics without attempting to fully cross the intersection.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player vehicle should move forward when SPACE is held and slow down when released. Traffic should continuously flow across the screen. The test is successful if the game runs without errors for at least 10 seconds and the vehicle responds to inputs correctly.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing a complete level progression by successfully crossing an intersection. This validates win conditions, level completion, coin rewards, and transition to the next level with increased difficulty.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The automated player will wait for a large gap in traffic (3+ seconds without nearby traffic), then hold SPACE continuously to accelerate across the intersection. After crossing, it will wait at the next starting position and repeat the strategy for multiple levels.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player should successfully cross at least 2-3 intersections without collision. Coins should be awarded after each crossing. The difficulty should increase with more traffic or faster speeds. Test is successful when multiple levels are completed and the score increases appropriately.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing collision detection and game over state. This validates that collisions with AI traffic are properly detected and trigger the correct game over sequence.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The automated player will intentionally drive into oncoming traffic by holding SPACE immediately at the start without waiting for gaps. This forces a collision within the first few seconds.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: A collision should be detected when the player vehicle contacts AI traffic. The game should immediately transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state, display the game over screen with final stats, and allow restart with R key. Test succeeds if collision detection works reliably.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing the pause and resume functionality during active gameplay. This ensures the pause mechanism correctly freezes physics and game state, then resumes properly.
TEST_4.strategy_description: The automated player will start moving forward, then trigger pause (ESC) after 3 seconds. After remaining paused for 2 seconds, it will unpause and continue moving. This tests state transitions and physics freezing.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: When paused, all vehicles should stop moving and physics should freeze. The pause overlay should display. Upon unpause, gameplay should resume from the exact state it was in. Test is successful if pause/unpause works smoothly without errors.

TEST_4.expected: When paused, all vehicles should stop moving and physics should freeze. The pause overlay should display. Upon unpause, gameplay should resume from the exact state it was in. Test is successful if pause/unpause works smoothly without errors.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing traffic density and speed scaling across multiple levels. This validates that difficulty progression works correctly with more challenging traffic patterns at higher levels.
TEST_5.strategy_description: The automated player will use safe crossing logic to complete 5+ levels in succession, measuring traffic density and speed at each level. It waits for gaps and crosses when safe.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Each subsequent level should have noticeably increased difficulty through faster traffic, more vehicles, or additional lanes. The player should be able to progress through at least 5 levels. Test succeeds if difficulty scaling is evident and progression feels balanced.

TEST_5.expected: Each subsequent level should have noticeably increased difficulty through faster traffic, more vehicles, or additional lanes. The player should be able to progress through at least 5 levels. Test succeeds if difficulty scaling is evident and progression feels balanced.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing edge cases including stopping at intersection boundaries and partial crossing attempts. This validates behavior when the player advances partway then retreats.
TEST_6.strategy_description: The automated player will tap SPACE briefly to move forward slightly, then release to stop in the middle of the intersection. It will repeat this stop-and-go pattern, testing partial movements and stopping mechanics.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: The player vehicle should be able to stop at any point in the intersection using braking. The vehicle should maintain its stopped position when SPACE is not held. Test succeeds if stop-and-go control works smoothly without physics glitches.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing restart functionality from game over state and proper reset of all game variables. This ensures clean game state when restarting after a collision.
TEST_7.strategy_description: The automated player will intentionally collide with traffic, wait in the game over state for 2 seconds, then press R to restart. After restart, it will attempt a successful crossing to verify the game reset properly.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: After pressing R, the game should return to START phase with all variables reset (score, level, etc.). The player should be able to press ENTER and play again successfully. Test succeeds if restart cleanly resets the game and allows normal gameplay.
